title: Top 10 LED Lights in India (2026)
description: The 10 best LED lights in India for 2026 - bulbs, panels, battens, downlights and floodlights. Wattage, lumens, CCT and BIS buying advice for bulk projects.
author: Elume Editorial
intro: LED lighting now powers almost every Indian home, office and site, but the catalogue is crowded and quality varies wildly between a genuine BIS-marked bulb and a cheap import that fades in a year. For contractors, builders and procurement managers buying by the carton, picking the right LED light is the difference between a clean handover and a stream of replacement calls. This 2026 guide ranks 10 dependable LED lights across bulbs, panels, battens, downlights and floodlights from the brands Indian projects actually trust.

Before you buy, weigh six things: wattage (power drawn), lumens (actual brightness), CCT or colour temperature (warm 3000K, neutral 4000K, cool 6500K), luminous efficacy in lm/W (higher means lower running cost), IP rating (IP65 for outdoor and wet areas), and warranty. Always confirm the BIS mark - it is mandatory for LED lighting in India and your first filter for genuine product.

items.name: Philips Ace Saver 9W LED Bulb
items.body: The 9W Philips Ace Saver is the default replacement for the old 60W incandescent and the single most-ordered SKU on most Indian sites. It delivers roughly 800-900 lumens at around 100 lm/W efficacy, runs cool to the touch and carries Philips' surge-protection circuitry that matters on unstable Indian grids. Available in cool daylight 6500K and warm white 3000K, it is BIS-marked and backed by a 2-year warranty. For bulk residential and PG handovers, it offers the best balance of brand recall, reliability and price. Order the cool white for kitchens and bathrooms, warm for bedrooms and lobbies.

items.name: Havells Adore 9W LED Bulb
items.body: Havells' Adore range is the go-to when a project specifies a premium Indian brand with strong after-sales reach. The 9W model pushes about 900 lumens at near 100 lm/W, with a wide-throw lens that lights rooms evenly rather than spotting. Its high-grade driver tolerates voltage swings from 100-290V, a real advantage in tier-2 and tier-3 sites. BIS-marked with a 2-year warranty and a dense service network, it is an easy specification for builders who want fewer warranty disputes. Stock both 6500K cool and 3000K warm; the 9W covers most rooms, with 12W for larger halls.

items.name: Syska SSK Rocket 9W LED Bulb
items.body: Syska's Rocket series is the value workhorse for high-volume, budget-sensitive jobs - PGs, hostels, affordable housing and rental blocks. The 9W bulb gives around 800 lumens at 6500K cool daylight, runs on a wide 100-300V input and is BIS-marked with up to a 2-year warranty depending on batch. It will not out-spec Philips on premium feel, but on cost-per-bulb at 15+ unit wholesale it is hard to beat for projects where the brief is sound, certified light at the lowest defensible price. A practical choice when the bill of quantities runs to hundreds of bulbs.

items.name: Wipro Garnet 22W LED Panel Light
items.body: For false-ceiling offices, clinics and showrooms, the Wipro Garnet recessed panel is a clean, glare-free choice. The 22W square and round versions deliver roughly 2000 lumens at about 90 lm/W with an even, edge-lit diffuser that avoids the dotty look of cheap panels. Available in 6500K cool and 4000K neutral, it suits commercial fit-outs where uniform brightness matters. BIS-marked with a 2-year warranty, it drops into standard 595mm and round cut-outs. For office projects, the 18-22W panel on a 600x600 grid is the workhorse; specify neutral 4000K for comfortable all-day work lighting.

items.name: Crompton Surya Neo 18W LED Panel
items.body: Crompton's Surya Neo slim panel is a reliable mid-range recessed light for offices, corridors and retail. The 18W model produces around 1700-1800 lumens at roughly 95 lm/W, with a uniform back-lit design and a low-profile body that fits shallow plenums. It comes in cool 6500K and neutral 4000K, is BIS-marked and carries a 2-year warranty. Crompton's wide distribution makes restocking on long-running projects straightforward. Pair the 18W for general areas with a 22-24W for higher ceilings, and keep CCT consistent across a floor to avoid patchy colour at handover.

items.name: Polycab Scintillate 20W LED Batten
items.body: The 4-foot LED batten remains the workhorse of kitchens, shops, garages and corridors, and Polycab's Scintillate is a sturdy pick. The 20W batten delivers around 2000 lumens at about 100 lm/W in 6500K cool daylight, with a slim aluminium-backed body that dissipates heat and extends life. BIS-marked with a 2-year warranty, it mounts surface or under-cabinet and links end-to-end for continuous runs. For commercial kitchens and warehouses where you need bright, shadow-free light over long stretches, the 20W batten at wholesale pricing is one of the most cost-effective lumens-per-rupee buys available.

items.name: Bajaj LEDZ 20W Batten
items.body: Bajaj's LEDZ batten is a dependable, value-focused alternative to Polycab for the same 4-foot tubelight slot. The 20W version puts out roughly 1800-2000 lumens at 6500K cool daylight, around 90-100 lm/W, on a wide-voltage driver suited to fluctuating supply. It is BIS-marked, carries a 2-year warranty and benefits from Bajaj's broad service footprint across smaller towns. For builders replacing legacy fluorescent tubes across an entire building, the LEDZ batten is a straight swap into existing wiring with no starter or choke, cutting both energy bills and maintenance call-backs.

items.name: Orient Eternal 12W LED Downlight
items.body: Recessed downlights set the tone for lobbies, hospitality and premium residential ceilings, and Orient's Eternal COB-style downlight is a clean performer. The 12W round model gives around 1000-1100 lumens with a tight, well-controlled beam and a low-glare recessed face, available in warm 3000K and cool 6500K. BIS-marked with a 2-year warranty, it fits standard 4-inch cut-outs and sits flush for a minimal look. Specify warm 3000K downlights for hotels, restaurants and living rooms where ambience matters; use the cool variant for task zones. A reliable mid-power spec for accent and general ceiling lighting.

items.name: Havells Jeta 50W LED Floodlight (IP65)
items.body: When the job moves outdoors - facades, gates, parking, factory yards - the Havells Jeta floodlight is a robust 2026 choice. The 50W model delivers around 4500-5000 lumens at roughly 90-100 lm/W in 6500K cool daylight, inside a die-cast aluminium body rated IP65 for dust and rain. BIS-marked with a 2-year warranty, its sealed driver and toughened glass handle Indian monsoons and dusty sites. For perimeter and security lighting, the 50W covers most mid-size areas; step up to 100-150W for large yards. Always insist on a genuine IP65 rating for any outdoor or exposed installation.

items.name: Crompton Mridang 50W LED Street Light (IP65)
items.body: For roads, campuses, layouts and large compounds, Crompton's Mridang street light closes out the list. The 50W pole-mount fixture produces around 5500-6000 lumens at strong efficacy near 110-120 lm/W in 6500K cool daylight, with a wide road-optic lens that spreads light along the carriageway rather than wasting it. Built to IP65 with surge protection and a die-cast housing, it is BIS-marked and warranty-backed. For developers and RWAs lighting internal roads and parking, the 50-72W street light range balances coverage, pole spacing and running cost; size wattage to pole height and span.

buyingTips: Buy on lumens and lm/W, not just watts. A genuine 9W bulb at 100 lm/W gives more usable light than a cheap 12W at 70 lm/W, while drawing less power - compare the lumen figure printed on the box, not the wattage alone.

Match CCT to the space and keep it consistent. Use warm 3000K for bedrooms, hotels and living areas; neutral 4000K for offices; cool 6500K for kitchens, shops, outdoors and security. Mixing colour temperatures on one floor looks patchy at handover.

Always confirm the BIS mark and IP rating before you order in bulk. BIS is mandatory for LED lighting in India and your first quality filter; insist on a true IP65 rating for any floodlight, street light or wet-area fixture.

Order at wholesale tiers and standardise SKUs. Consolidating to a few proven models at 15+ units unlocks Elume wholesale pricing, simplifies warranty claims, and means restocking and replacements stay identical across a long-running project.

faq.q: How many watts of LED do I need to replace an old bulb?
faq.a: A 9W LED bulb (around 800-900 lumens) replaces a 60W incandescent, and a 12W LED replaces a 75-100W bulb. Always check lumens rather than watts: aim for roughly 800 lumens for general room lighting and more for kitchens or task areas.

faq.q: What is the difference between 3000K, 4000K and 6500K?
faq.a: These are colour temperatures. 3000K is warm yellow light for bedrooms, hotels and living rooms; 4000K is neutral white, ideal for offices; 6500K is cool daylight, best for kitchens, shops, security and outdoor areas. Pick one CCT per zone and keep it consistent.

faq.q: Do LED lights for outdoor use need a special rating?
faq.a: Yes. Any outdoor or wet-area fixture - floodlights, street lights, garden and facade lighting - should be IP65 rated, meaning dust-tight and protected against water jets. A non-rated indoor light will fail quickly in monsoon or dusty site conditions, so always confirm IP65 before ordering.

faq.q: Is the BIS mark mandatory for LED lights in India?
faq.a: Yes. BIS certification is mandatory for LED bulbs, battens, panels and most luminaires sold in India under the relevant standards. The BIS mark is your first check for a genuine, safety-tested product - avoid any bulk LED purchase that cannot show valid BIS marking on the packaging.

faq.q: What warranty should I expect on LED lights for a project?
faq.a: Reputable Indian brands like Philips, Havells, Crompton, Wipro, Polycab and Bajaj offer 2-year warranties on most LED bulbs, battens and panels, with some floodlights and street lights covered similarly. Standardising on a few branded SKUs makes warranty claims far simpler across a long-running project.
